public_state: Magneton @ Air Balloon is active with Spikes / Thunderbolt / Thunder Wave / Explosion. The player lead plan was to click a Ground move.
gsc_transfer: Ground pressure is normally the clean Electric punish.
romhack_rewrite: Air Balloon can invalidate the first Ground hit, so the turn is about popping Balloon, denying Spikes/status, and avoiding a bad Explosion trade.
expected_candidate_classes: direct hit to pop Balloon or force Magneton
line that denies Spikes or Thunder Wave on an irreplaceable piece

public_state: Gligar @ Quick Claw is active with Spikes / Earthquake / Wing Attack / Toxic. The obvious switch is also the best Kingdra answer.
gsc_transfer: Preserve the answer to the setup win condition.
romhack_rewrite: Do not spend the Dragon Dance answer just to absorb Gligar unless it denies Spikes/Toxic and remains healthy enough for Kingdra/Dragonair.
expected_candidate_classes: status/hazard absorber with lower future job value

public_state: Ariados @ Leftovers is active with Spikes / Toxic / Leech Life / Spider Web. The player lead can set up but does not threaten Ariados quickly.
gsc_transfer: Do not donate free long-game support turns.
romhack_rewrite: Ariados can turn a passive setup turn into Spikes, Toxic, or trap ownership before Koga's anchors arrive.
expected_candidate_classes: pivot before trap/status if the active is irreplaceable
setup only if it converts before Ariados's clock

public_state: Alakazam @ Choice Specs reveals Psychic. The player's current Pokemon survives; a bench answer may exploit the lock.
gsc_transfer: Punish locked or predictable special pressure.
romhack_rewrite: Choice Specs is both a damage amplifier and a commitment; after the revealed Psychic, ranking must include damage survival and the next-board lock punish.
expected_candidate_classes: stay and make immediate progress if survival route matters
mechanics_gates: damage range 119-141 into Muk from anchor check

public_state: Pidgeot @ Choice Band is active with Wing Attack / Double-Edge / Steel Wing / Quick Attack unrevealed.
gsc_transfer: Scout and punish commitment when the opponent has a narrow strong opener.
romhack_rewrite: The first hit defines a Choice Band lock, so the answer must cover immediate damage and the next-board owner after the lock.

public_state: Cloyster @ NeverMeltIce is active with Spikes / Surf / Ice Beam / Explosion. The player can set hazards or attack.
gsc_transfer: Spiker plus Explosion is a route trade, not just support plus damage.
romhack_rewrite: Cloyster can create hazard pressure and then cash out; the player must decide whether attacking, hazarding, or pivoting handles both jobs.
expected_candidate_classes: deny first Spikes layer or make it costly
attack to force Cloyster survival decision

public_state: Muk @ Rocky Helmet is active with Flamethrower / Sludge Bomb / Sleep Talk / Rest. The player's strongest immediate damage is contact physical.
gsc_transfer: Do not spend a key attacker into a wall unless the exchange converts.
romhack_rewrite: Rocky Helmet/contact, Poison passive risk, and RestTalk reset can make contact damage fake progress.
expected_candidate_classes: non-contact damage or status/control if legal
contact attack with Helmet/passive/Rest consequences priced

public_state: Gyarados @ Leftovers threatens Dragon Dance with Outrage / Surf / Hyper Beam available.
gsc_transfer: Setup denial must happen before the boosted route owns the game.
romhack_rewrite: Dragon Dance uses local bestattackup plus Speed, and Outrage category can change for Dragon users; denial, phaze/Haze/status/revenge, and commitment punish must all be considered.
expected_candidate_classes: Outrage or Hyper Beam commitment punish if setup happens

public_state: Exeggutor @ Life Orb has Psychic / Giga Drain / Sleep Powder / Explosion. The player has one obvious sleep absorber and one important long-game answer.
gsc_transfer: Sleep, recovery, and Explosion are future-turn route resources.
romhack_rewrite: Life Orb damage/recoil, Giga Drain recovery, Sleep Powder, and Explosion all compete; the answer must say what route each branch buys or denies.
expected_candidate_classes: sleep absorber if the absorber's job is expendable
immediate pressure that denies sleep/cash-out value

public_state: Forretress @ Leftovers has Spikes / Protect / Toxic / Explosion. Starmie spinner is still alive or unrevealed.
gsc_transfer: Hazard progress must be connected to retention and follow-up pressure.
romhack_rewrite: Forretress can be hazard setter, poison clock, scout, or cash-out piece; Starmie can later erase hazard progress with Rapid Spin.
expected_candidate_classes: line that punishes or accounts for Starmie Spin

public_state: Snorlax @ Leftovers has Curse / Sleep Talk / Rest / Body Slam. It can enter as a long-game anchor.
gsc_transfer: The Snorlax lesson is anchor mapping and reset-cycle control.
romhack_rewrite: The answer must name the local boosted-Body-Slam answer, controller for Rest/Sleep Talk turns, and the line that prevents Curse from becoming irreversible.
expected_candidate_classes: preserve the boosted Body Slam answer

public_state: Steelix is Steel/Dragon with Earthquake / Iron Tail / Outrage / Roar, and Spikes are up.
gsc_transfer: Do not pivot from type slogans; check the route and local chart.
romhack_rewrite: Local Steelix is Steel/Dragon, can Roar through Spikes, and uses Outrage under local Dragon mechanics; vanilla Steelix assumptions are unsafe.
